title: 9 Best Herbal Teas For Heartburn
intro: Herbal teas for Heartburn are natural remedies that involve infusing the leaves, roots, or flowers of various herbs in hot water to create a soothing and calming drink, aimed at alleviating the symptoms of heartburn. This ailment occurs when stomach acid flows back up into the esophagus, causing a burning sensation in the chest. Herbal teas can effectively treat heartburn due to their anti-inflammatory and calming properties, which help to reduce inflammation and relax the muscles in the esophagus, thereby alleviating the discomfort. Examples of herbal teas that can help treat heartburn include chamomile, which soothes the digestive system, peppermint, which relaxes the muscles and reduces inflammation, licorice root, which coats the esophagus and protects it from acid, ginger, which reduces inflammation and nausea, and slippery elm, which forms a protective barrier in the esophagus to prevent acid from flowing back up.

preparations.herb_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, derived from the roots of the licorice plant, have been traditionally used to treat heartburn ailments due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat heartburn by reducing inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, thereby alleviating symptoms such as acid reflux and burning sensations. The bioactive constituents of Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, including glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, play a crucial role in reducing inflammation and protecting the mucous membranes from acid damage. The benefits of using Glycyrrhiza glabra teas to treat heartburn include natural relief from symptoms, improved digestion, and reduced risk of complications associated with long-term heartburn.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Glycyrrhiza glabra roots or 2 teaspoons of dried roots.
Boil 1 cup of water and let it cool for 5 minutes.
Add 1-2 teaspoons of the Glycyrrhiza glabra roots to the cooled water.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes. Strain the liquid and discard the solids.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea 30 minutes before meals to help prevent heartburn.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the study on "Microbial pathogenesis", Glycyrrhiza glabra teas for heartburn may help alleviate symptoms due to the antimicrobial properties demonstrated by extracts from Glycyrrhiza glabra against Helicobacter pylori, which can contribute to heartburn.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can be an effective remedy for heartburn, but its prolonged use may cause side effects such as headaches, dizziness, and changes in blood pressure. To use Glycyrrhiza glabra teas safely, it's essential to start with low doses and gradually increase as needed, and to avoid consuming it for extended periods to minimize the risk of potassium depletion and other complications.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Glycyrrhiza glabra tea 1-2 times a day
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried root per cup of boiling water
Steep for 5-7 minutes and strain the tea
Take 1/2 to 1 teaspoon of dried root 3 times a day as needed
Use for up to 2 weeks, then take a 2-week break

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, also known as ginger tea, have been traditionally used to treat heartburn and other digestive issues due to their anti-inflammatory and carminative properties. The bioactive constituents of ginger tea, including gingerols and shogaols, help to reduce inflammation in the esophagus and alleviate symptoms of heartburn by relaxing the lower esophageal sphincter. This herbal preparation helps to treat heartburn by reducing the frequency and severity of acid reflux episodes, providing quick relief from burning sensations and discomfort. The benefits of using Zingiber officinale teas to treat heartburn include reduced symptoms, improved digestion, and a lower risk of complications such as esophagitis and Barrett's esophagus.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Zingiber officinale root and 1 cup of boiling water.
Add the Zingiber officinale root to a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Pour the boiling water over the root and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and add 1 tablespoon of honey to taste, if desired.
Drink the tea as needed for heartburn relief, up to 3 times a day.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Pakistan journal of pharmaceutical sciences", Zingiber officinale teas may be beneficial for heartburn due to its high antioxidant activity, as it contains significant levels of total phenolic compounds, flavonoids, phenolic acids, and polyphenols.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can be used to treat heartburn, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, diarrhea, and dizziness in some individuals, especially when consumed in large quantities or in combination with other medications. To use Zingiber officinale teas safely, it is recommended to start with a small dose, gradually increase as needed, and avoid taking it with other stimulants like caffeine and spicy foods.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Zingiber officinale tea for heartburn.
Take 1/2 to 1 teaspoon of dried Zingiber officinale root powder per cup of tea.
Drink 1 cup of Zingiber officinale tea 2-3 times a day as needed.
Start with small dosages and adjust as directed by your doctor.
Continue using Zingiber officinale tea for 2-4 weeks to see noticeable results.

preparations.herb_desc: Foeniculum vulgare teas, also known as fennel tea, have been used for centuries to treat heartburn and other digestive issues due to their anti-inflammatory and carminative properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat heartburn by reducing inflammation in the esophagus and alleviating symptoms such as acid reflux and bloating. Fennel tea contains bioactive constituents such as anethole, fenchone, and limonene, which have been shown to relax the muscles in the digestive tract and reduce the production of stomach acid. Regular consumption of fennel tea has been found to provide relief from heartburn symptoms and promote overall digestive health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Foeniculum vulgare (fennel) leaves and flowers.
Combine 1 tablespoon of fennel with 1 cup of boiling water in a teapot.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain it.
Add honey to taste, if desired. You can also add a pinch of salt.
Drink the tea 1-2 times a day to help with heartburn relief.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Foeniculum vulgare teas can be used to treat heartburn due to its antispasmodic and carminative properties, but it may cause side effects such as digestive discomfort, bloating, and allergic reactions in some individuals. Precautions should be taken when using Foeniculum vulgare teas, including limiting consumption to small amounts and avoiding use in individuals with sensitive stomachs, pregnant or breastfeeding women, and those with allergies to plants in the parsley family.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Foeniculum vulgare tea after meals to help with heartburn
Take 1-2 teaspoons of dried Foeniculum vulgare leaves per cup of boiling water
Steep for 5-7 minutes and strain the tea before drinking
Drink 2-3 cups of Foeniculum vulgare tea per day as needed for heartburn relief
Consult a doctor or pharmacist for guidance on using Foeniculum vulgare tea for heartburn

preparations.herb_desc: Mentha x piperita teas, also known as peppermint tea, have been traditionally used to treat heartburn due to its soothing and calming properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat heartburn by reducing inflammation in the esophagus and relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract, thereby alleviating discomfort and pain. The bioactive constituents of Mentha x piperita, including menthol and menthone, have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which contribute to its heartburn-relieving properties. The benefits of using Mentha x piperita teas to treat heartburn include its natural and non-invasive nature, making it a popular alternative to pharmaceutical treatments for many individuals.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Mentha x piperita leaves.
Heat 1 cup of water in a pot until it starts boiling.
Add 2 tablespoons of fresh or dried Mentha x piperita leaves to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes to release the tea's properties.
Strain the tea and drink 1 cup, 30 minutes before meals to help with heartburn.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Current pharmaceutical biotechnology", Mentha x piperita teas for heartburn have been found to be effective in managing visceral hypersensitivity, particularly in irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and dyspepsia, through modulation of TRPV1 and suppression of serotonin receptors.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Mentha x piperita teas can be beneficial in treating heartburn, but possible side effects may include stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions such as hives or itching. Precautions to take when using Mentha x piperita teas for heartburn treatment include avoiding large doses, as this may exacerbate stomach issues, and being cautious if you have sensitive skin or are prone to allergic reactions.
preparation_usage: Drink 1/4 cup of Mentha x piperita tea after meals
Use 1 teaspoon of dried Mentha x piperita leaves per cup
Repeat tea 2-3 times a day as needed for heartburn
Start with 1-2 days of usage and adjust as needed
Consult doctor before using tea for prolonged periods

preparations.herb_desc: Cinnamomum verum teas, derived from the bark of the Ceylon cinnamon tree, have been traditionally used to alleviate heartburn symptoms. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of this herbal preparation help to soothe the digestive tract, reduce inflammation, and ease discomfort associated with heartburn. The bioactive constituents, including cinnamaldehyde and eugenol, work together to relax the muscles in the esophagus, reduce acid production in the stomach, and enhance digestion, ultimately providing relief from heartburn symptoms. By incorporating Cinnamomum verum teas into one's diet, individuals can benefit from the natural and non-invasive treatment of heartburn, promoting a healthier digestive system and overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of Cinnamomum verum bark, a cup of water, and a strainer.
Boil the water in a pot and add the Cinnamomum verum bark.
Reduce heat to simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup and let it cool.
Drink the tea 30 minutes before meals to help prevent heartburn.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cinnamomum verum teas can provide relief for heartburn sufferers, but it may also cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea due to its high cinnamon oil content. To minimize potential risks, it is recommended to consume Cinnamomum verum teas in moderation, ideally after meals, and to avoid excessive consumption, especially in people with sensitive stomachs.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Cinnamomum verum tea after meals 2 times a day
Start with 1/2 teaspoon of dried leaves per cup of boiling water
Gradually increase to 1 teaspoon per cup if symptoms persist
Use 2-3 cups per day, as needed, for 1-2 weeks
Monitor symptoms and consult a doctor if heartburn worsens

preparations.herb_desc: Cinchona officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat heartburn, a common digestive disorder characterized by the backflow of stomach acid into the esophagus. The anti-inflammatory and astringent properties of this herbal preparation help to treat heartburn by reducing inflammation in the esophagus and preventing the backflow of stomach acid. The bioactive constituents of Cinchona officinalis, including alkaloids such as quinine and cinchonine, help to relax the muscles in the esophagus and reduce acid production in the stomach, thereby alleviating heartburn symptoms. The benefits of using Cinchona officinalis teas to treat heartburn include reduced discomfort and pain, improved digestion, and a decrease in the risk of complications associated with chronic heartburn.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Cinchona officinalis bark and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the bark in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid into a cup and discard the bark.
Add 1 teaspoon of honey (optional) to the liquid.
Drink the tea once or twice a day as needed to help with heartburn.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cinchona officinalis teas can be used to treat heartburn due to their anti-inflammatory properties, but possible side effects may include stomach upset, nausea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. Precautions to take when using Cinchona officinalis teas to treat heartburn include starting with small doses, avoiding consumption on an empty stomach, and being cautious of potential interactions with other medications or health conditions.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of warm water 30 minutes before meals.
Take 1/2 teaspoon of dried Cinchona officinalis powder.
Mix with 1 cup of warm water, stir well, and drink.
Repeat dosage every 2 hours as needed, up to 3 times a day.
Use for 1-2 weeks to manage heartburn symptoms effectively.

preparations.herb_desc: Curcuma longa teas, also known as turmeric tea, have been traditionally used to treat heartburn and its associated symptoms. The anti-inflammatory and carminative properties of Curcuma longa teas help to reduce inflammation in the esophagus and alleviate heartburn symptoms. The bioactive constituents, including curcumin, demethoxycurcumin, and bisdemethoxycurcumin, in Curcuma longa teas have been found to inhibit the production of stomach acid and relax the lower esophageal sphincter, thereby reducing heartburn symptoms. The benefits of using Curcuma longa teas to treat heartburn include reduced frequency and severity of symptoms, improved digestion, and a lower risk of complications associated with chronic heartburn.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried turmeric root (Curcuma longa) and 1 cup of boiling water.
Add the turmeric root to a tea infuser or a small piece of cheesecloth.
Steep the turmeric root in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the turmeric root.
Drink the tea warm or at room temperature, 1-2 times a day as needed.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the provided study, Curcuma longa teas for heartburn may be beneficial due to the presence of curcumin, a compound that is part of the phytochemical analysis of Curcuma longa, which has potential in preventing or treating gastrointestinal conditions.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Curcuma longa teas can be an effective herbal remedy for heartburn, but potential side effects may include stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions such as skin rashes or itching. Precautions should be taken when using Curcuma longa teas to treat heartburn, including avoiding excessive consumption, as it can exacerbate digestive issues, and being cautious with interactions with other medications, especially blood thinners and diabetes medications.
preparation_usage: Drink 1/2 cup of Curcuma longa tea after meals to prevent heartburn.
Start with 1/2 teaspoon of Curcuma longa powder per 8 oz of water.
Increase dosage to 1 teaspoon per 8 oz of water if needed.
Consume 2-3 cups of Curcuma longa tea per day for heartburn relief.
Drink tea 30 minutes before meals to help reduce heartburn symptoms.

preparations.herb_desc: Zanthoxylum bungeanum teas have been traditionally used to treat heartburn due to its carminative and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to alleviate symptoms such as bloating and discomfort. The herbal preparation helps to treat heartburn by reducing inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, promoting digestive health, and regulating the movement of food through the digestive system. The bioactive constituents of Zanthoxylum bungeanum teas, including volatile oils and phenolic compounds, contribute to its therapeutic effects by relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract and reducing the production of stomach acid. Regular consumption of Zanthoxylum bungeanum teas has been found to provide relief from heartburn symptoms and promote overall digestive well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Zanthoxylum bungeanum leaves, 1 cup of water, and a tea infuser or strainer.
Rinse the leaves with cold water and drain well.
Place 1 tablespoon of the leaves in the tea infuser or strainer.
Steep the leaves in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink 1 cup 2-3 times a day to help with heartburn.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zanthoxylum bungeanum teas can help alleviate heartburn symptoms, but may also cause side effects such as dizziness, headaches, or stomach upset in some individuals due to its potential to interact with certain medications or exacerbate existing conditions like acid reflux. Precautions should be taken when using Zanthoxylum bungeanum teas to treat heartburn, including avoiding excessive consumption, as it may lead to an imbalance of essential nutrients, and being cautious when combining it with other herbal remedies or supplements.
preparation_usage: Drink 1-2 cups of Zanthoxylum bungeanum tea after meals
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried herb per cup of boiling water
Use 2-3 times a day as needed for heartburn relief
Adjust dosage to 1-2 teaspoons per cup for severe heartburn
Consult a doctor if heartburn persists or worsens after 3 days

preparations.herb_desc: Silybum marianum teas, also known as milk thistle teas, have been traditionally used to treat heartburn, a common gastrointestinal disorder. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of this herbal preparation help to soothe the digestive tract, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of heartburn. The bioactive constituents, including silymarin and flavonoids, play a crucial role in treating heartburn by protecting the liver, enhancing the production of bile, and improving digestion. The benefits of Silybum marianum teas in treating heartburn include reduced frequency and severity of symptoms, improved quality of life, and a natural alternative to over-the-counter medications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Silybum marianum (milk thistle) flowers and leaves.
Combine 1 tablespoon of dried Silybum marianum with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup.
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the mixture using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Discard the solids and store the tea in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.
Drink 1/2 to 1 cup of the tea, 30 minutes to 1 hour before meals to help alleviate heartburn symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Silybum marianum teas can be effective in treating heartburn, but possible side effects may include gastrointestinal upset, such as nausea, diarrhea, and abdominal pain, as well as allergic reactions like skin rashes and itching. Precautions should be taken when using Silybum marianum teas, including starting with small doses to assess individual tolerance, avoiding prolonged use, and being cautious when combining with other medications, especially blood thinners and diabetes medications.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Silybum marianum tea after meals to reduce heartburn
Use 1-2 teaspoons of dried Silybum marianum leaves per cup of boiling water
Steep for 5-10 minutes to allow the herbs to infuse
Drink 2-3 cups of tea per day, spaced out over 2-3 hours
Continue usage for 7-14 days to see optimal results

The bioactive constituents of piper nigrum teas, including piperine and other alkaloids, help to reduce inflammation and relax the muscles in the digestive tract, allowing for easier digestion and alleviation of heartburn symptoms. By incorporating piper nigrum teas into one's diet, individuals can benefit from its natural anti-inflammatory and digestive-aiding properties to effectively treat heartburn and promote overall digestive health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water and 1 teaspoon of dried Piper nigrum seeds.
Heat the water in a pot until it boils.
Add the dried Piper nigrum seeds to the boiling water.
Reduce heat to low and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink it 30 minutes before eating to help prevent heartburn.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Piper nigrum teas can be used to treat heartburn, but its use may lead to side effects such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and digestive issues like diarrhea or stomach cramps. To use Piper nigrum teas safely, take precautions to avoid consuming excessive amounts, which can cause increased heart rate and blood pressure, and also be cautious of its interaction with other medications, especially blood thinners and antacids.

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber zerumbet teas have been traditionally used to treat heartburn, a common condition characterized by a burning sensation in the chest and throat. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of this herbal preparation help to soothe the digestive tract, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms associated with heartburn. The bioactive constituents of Zingiber zerumbet teas, including gingerols and shogaols, have been shown to have gastroprotective effects, reducing acid production and improving digestion. The benefits of using Zingiber zerumbet teas to treat heartburn include improved digestion, reduced inflammation, and a decrease in symptoms associated with acid reflux.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Zingiber zerumbet roots and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the Zingiber zerumbet roots in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup.
Add honey or sugar to taste, if needed, and mix well.
Drink the tea 1-2 times a day to help alleviate heartburn symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber zerumbet teas can cause gastrointestinal upset, such as bloating, diarrhea, and stomach discomfort, in some individuals when used to treat heartburn, although these side effects are generally mild and temporary. To minimize potential side effects, it is recommended to start with a small dose, gradually increasing as needed, and to avoid consuming Zingiber zerumbet teas on an empty stomach.

preparations.herb_desc: Zataria multiflora teas, also known as hyssop teas, have been traditionally used to treat heartburn and other digestive issues. The anti-inflammatory and antacid properties of this herbal preparation help to treat heartburn by reducing inflammation and neutralizing stomach acid. The bioactive constituents of Zataria multiflora, including thujone, borneol, and pinene, have been shown to relax the muscles in the stomach and reduce symptoms of heartburn. Regular consumption of Zataria multiflora teas has been found to provide relief from heartburn and other digestive issues, making it a popular natural remedy for those suffering from acid reflux.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Zataria multiflora leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid into a cup to remove the leaves.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to sweeten the tea, if desired.
Drink 1 cup of the tea, 1-2 times a day, as needed for heartburn relief.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zataria multiflora teas can be an effective herbal remedy for treating heartburn, but possible side effects may include stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions such as hives or itching. To use Zataria multiflora teas safely, it is recommended to start with a small dose, avoid taking it with other medications that may interact with it, and be cautious when consuming it if you have a history of stomach problems or allergies.

preparations.herb_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis teas, also known as rosemary tea, have been used traditionally to treat heartburn and other digestive issues due to their carminative and antacid properties. The bioactive constituents of rosemary tea, including rosmarinic acid and carnosic acid, help to reduce inflammation and protect the mucous membranes of the esophagus, thereby alleviating heartburn symptoms. Drinking rosemary tea can help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reduce acid production, and improve digestion, making it an effective natural remedy for heartburn. The benefits of rosemary tea in treating heartburn include its ability to provide quick relief, promote relaxation, and support overall digestive health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Rosmarinus officinalis leaves.
Heat 1 cup of water in a pot.
Pour the water over the Rosmarinus officinalis leaves and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the leaves.
Drink the tea when it's still warm to help soothe heartburn symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the provided study, Rosmarinus officinalis teas for heartburn may contain compounds such as phenolics and flavonoids, which are part of the main active substances within this plant, and have potential to alleviate digestive problems, including heartburn.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Rosmarinus officinalis teas can be beneficial in treating heartburn due to their anti-inflammatory properties, but possible side effects may include stomach upset, nausea, and dizziness, especially when consumed in large amounts or over an extended period. To use Rosmarinus officinalis teas safely, take small amounts and dilute them with water, avoid consuming them before bedtime to prevent nighttime discomfort, and be cautious when combining them with other medications or supplements.
